mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects robust manufacturing and utility output capacity, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above its 12-month MA coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with a rising unemployment rate create stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive conditions longer, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate profit pools, establishing a positive baseline relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals a sudden labor market contraction that reduces wage growth expectations and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following a payroll decline exceeding 50,000 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5% forces markets to price in prolonged monetary restriction and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.5% coincides with positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during tightening regimes.
